summary: Israeli forces of the 36th Division have crossed the Litani River and are operating across southern Lebanon, while Hezbollah rockets launched from Lebanon were largely intercepted by Israeli defenses. Iran reported continued air‑defence activity on Qeshm Island and rejected U.S. claims of Iranian aggression in the Strait of Hormuz. Multiple border alerts were issued in northern Israel with several rocket launches from Lebanon intercepted, causing no casualties.
key_points: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu confirmed IDF 36th Division crossed the Litani River and advanced toward strategic points in southern Lebanon (Source: @manniefabian/58307).
Hezbollah rockets fired from Lebanon were detected and mostly intercepted, with a fall observed near Kiryat Shmona and no injuries reported (Source: @New_security8200/200240).
Iran’s Hormuz authority condemned U.S. sanctions and stated they will not enable Washington to control the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @presstv/191918).
Iranian state media confirmed air‑defence activity on Qeshm Island in Hormozgan Province (Source: @shin_persian/6307).
Israeli military issued evacuation warnings for villages in southern Lebanon, including Jdeidet Ansar and Mazra’at Kutharia (Source: @N12chat/209929).
A senior Iranian lieutenant was reported killed by shrapnel on Qeshm Island (Source: @shin_persian/6312).
U.S. forces reported damage to two MQ‑9 drones at Al‑Ula‑Salm base after intercepting an Iranian ballistic missile over Kuwait (Source: @N12chat/209926).

sections.executive_summary: Israeli forces of the 36th Division have crossed the Litani River and are operating across southern Lebanon, striking Hezbollah positions. Hezbollah launched rockets toward northern Israel, most of which were intercepted, causing no casualties. Iran reported air‑defence activity on Qeshm Island and rejected U.S. sanctions on the Strait of Hormuz.

points: Iran’s Strait of Hormuz authority condemned U.S. sanctions, stating they will not help Washington gain control of the strategic waterway (Source: @presstv/191918).
State‑run Mehr News confirmed air‑defence activity on Qeshm Island in Hormozgan Province (Source: @shin_persian/6307).
An Iranian lieutenant was reported killed by shrapnel on Qeshm Island, indicating active combat or accident there (Source: @shin_persian/6312).
A video showed wreckage of a UAV on Qeshm Island, confirming aerial incidents in the area (Source: @shin_persian/6314).

Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced that IDF 36th Division crossed the Litani River and advanced toward dominating terrain in southern Lebanon, also operating in Beirut and the Beqaa (Source: @manniefabian/58307).
Israeli military reported multiple rocket launches from Lebanon toward northern Israel; most were intercepted, with a fall near Kiryat Shmona and no casualties (Source: @New_security8200/200240).
The IDF spokesperson announced the destruction of a Hezbollah rocket launcher that fired rockets toward northern Israel during the night (Source: @New_security8200/200247).
The IDF issued evacuation warnings for villages in southern Lebanon, including Jdeidet Ansar, Mazra’at Kutharia, Al‑Zararia, and Mishghara (Source: @N12chat/209929).
Israeli forces reported killing Hamas operatives in Gaza, including a senior commander linked to the October 7 attacks (Source: @inon_yttach/8389).
Israeli forces in the southern Gaza sector reported killing three armed Hamas militants threatening IDF units (Source: @idf_telegram/23965).
The UN spokesperson affirmed that 100% of the Gaza Strip belongs to the Palestinian people, rejecting Israeli claims of annexation (Source: @SharghDaily/162756).
Hezbollah rockets were launched toward northern Israel, with most intercepted and one impact near Kiryat Shmona, causing no injuries (Source: @New_security8200/200240).
Israeli evacuation warnings were issued for several Lebanese villages in the western Bekaa region (Source: @N12chat/209929).
A Lebanese post reported a “detonation operation” in the Marjeyoun area and bombings in Dbayeh, Blaat, and Jamijma (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/363304).
U.S. forces reported damage to two MQ‑9 drones at Al‑Ula‑Salm base after intercepting an Iranian ballistic missile over Kuwait (Source: @N12chat/209926).
American troops were noted as having minor injuries following the missile interception over Kuwait (Source: @N12chat/209928).
Iranian officials rejected U.S. claims of a breakthrough in ending the “war of aggression” and said message exchanges with Washington continue (Source: @presstv/191920).
U.S. sanctions on the Strait of Hormuz were condemned by Iran’s Hormuz authority as ineffective (Source: @presstv/191918).

key_developments: Israeli 36th Division crossed the Litani River and advanced toward strategic points in southern Lebanon, also operating in Beirut and the Beqaa (Source: @manniefabian/58307).
Hezbollah launched rockets toward northern Israel; most were intercepted, with a fall near Kiryat Shmona and no casualties (Source: @New_security8200/200240).
Iran’s Hormuz authority condemned U.S. sanctions, stating they will not enable control of the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @presstv/191918).
Iranian state media confirmed air‑defence activity on Qeshm Island in Hormozgan Province (Source: @shin_persian/6307).
An Iranian lieutenant was reported killed by shrapnel on Qeshm Island (Source: @shin_persian/6312).
U.S. forces reported damage to two MQ‑9 drones after intercepting an Iranian ballistic missile over Kuwait (Source: @N12chat/209926).
The IDF issued evacuation warnings for villages in southern Lebanon, including Jdeidet Ansar and Mazra’at Kutharia (Source: @N12chat/209929).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah continues to launch rockets toward northern Israel, but Israeli air‑defence systems are effectively intercepting most threats, limiting casualties. Iran maintains a defensive posture in the Strait of Hormuz and on Qeshm Island, indicating readiness to counter regional pressure. U.S. military assets in the region face emerging missile threats, as shown by the intercepted ballistic missile over Kuwait.
sections.regional_response: Israel is expanding ground operations in southern Lebanon while reinforcing northern border defenses. Iran is publicly rejecting U.S. diplomatic overtures and sanctions, while bolstering air‑defence capabilities on Qeshm Island. The United States is maintaining a presence in the Gulf, responding to missile threats and continuing sanctions against Iran.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Hezbollah rocket launch sites in southern Lebanon should be intensified to pre‑empt further attacks. Continued surveillance of Iranian air‑defence activity on Qeshm Island is required to assess escalation risk in the Persian Gulf. U.S. drone vulnerability to Iranian missile capabilities warrants review of operational security protocols in the region.
